body.title: agent_datacenter: IMAP mailbox created on device registration, retained on deregistration
body.result: Skeleton.__init__ accepts optional imap_server param. register_device() calls imap_server.create_mailbox() after registry write (idempotent). deregister_device() calls set_status('offline') — mailbox NOT deleted (24hr retention).
body.description:
Hook device registration/deregistration to IMAP mailbox lifecycle. Mailboxes persist after deregistration for log retention.

Proposed shape:
- In Skeleton.register_device(): after registry write, call imap_server.create_mailbox(device_id)
  If mailbox already exists (device reattach): no-op (idempotent)
- In Skeleton.deregister_device(): do NOT call delete_mailbox — mailbox persists for 24hr log retention
  Update registry status to 'offline'; mailbox stays browseable
- Manual cleanup: agentctl cleanup-mailboxes (future ticket; out of scope here)

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/skeleton.py (register_device hook)

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: comment in register_device explains why mailbox is created here and why delete is not called on deregistration

Scope boundary:
IN: mailbox creation on registration, no-delete on deregistration
OUT: 24hr retention policy (T-adc-imap-24hr-retention), Shared mailbox (created in IMAPServer.start())

Test plan:
- Register device 'igor' → imap mailbox 'igor' created
- Deregister 'igor' → imap mailbox 'igor' still exists
- Register 'igor' again (reattach) → no RegistrationError; mailbox still has old messages
